title: Kitchen appliance and method
abstract: A kitchen appliance (100) is disclosed comprising a compartment (110) for receiving flour, said compartment comprising a sensor (120) for producing a sensor reading indicative of a dielectric property of said flour or a product comprising said flour. The kitchen appliance further comprises a processing arrangement (130) communicatively coupled to the sensor for determining an initial moisture content of said flour or product from said sensor reading and adapted to generate a control signal indicative of an amount of water to be added to the compartment as a function of the determined moisture content. A method of controlling a flour product-based food preparation process is also disclosed.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A kitchen appliance (100) comprising: a compartment (110) for receiving flour, said compartment comprising a sensor (120) for producing a sensor reading indicative of a dielectric property of said flour or a product comprising said flour; and a processing arrangement (130) communicatively coupled to the sensor for determining an initial moisture content of said flour or product from said sensor reading and adapted to generate a control signal indicative of an amount of water to be added to the compartment as a function of the determined initial moisture content. 2. The kitchen appliance of claim 1, wherein the kitchen appliance comprises a user interface (105) including a display screen, wherein the control signal is for generating a water addition instruction on said display screen. 3. The kitchen appliance of claim 1, further comprising a water inlet (140) responsive to said control signal. 4. The kitchen appliance (100) of any of claims 1-3, wherein the processing arrangement (130) is adapted to generate said control signal as a function of a series of said dielectric property sensor readings. 5. The kitchen appliance (100) of any of claims 1-4, wherein the sensor (120) is located in a floor (112) of the compartment (110) or above the flour or product or in a mixing element (114) of the kitchen appliance. 6. The kitchen appliance (100) of any of claims 1-5, wherein the sensor (120) comprises a probe for transmitting a radio-frequency signal and for receiving a reflection of said signal, said reflection corresponding to said dielectric property. 7. The kitchen appliance (100) of claim 6, wherein the probe is a coaxial probe comprising a coaxial line for transmitting the radio-frequency signal and a flange adjacent said coaxial line for receiving said reflection. 8. The kitchen appliance (100) of any of claims 1-7, wherein the processing arrangement (130) is arranged to generate the control signal based on a user preference of a property of a food product to be produced from the flour or product. 9. The kitchen appliance (100) of any of claims 1-8, wherein the processing arrangement (130) is programmable to receive a correlation between the sensor reading and initial moisture content. 10. The kitchen appliance (100) of any of claims 1-9, wherein the processing arrangement (130) comprises a detection stage (132) for detecting the sensor reading, a determination stage (134) for determining the initial moisture content from the detected sensor reading and a water adding stage (136) for generating the control signal. 11. The kitchen appliance (100) of any of claims 1-10, wherein the processing arrangement (130) is further adapted to operate the kitchen appliance as a function of the sensor reading. 12. The kitchen appliance (100) of claim 11, further comprising a heating unit (150), wherein the processing arrangement (130) is further adapted to determine the amount of water bound to the flour or product from the sensor reading and to generate a further control signal for controlling an amount of power supplied to the heating unit (150) as a function of the determined amount of bound water. 13. The kitchen appliance (100) of any of claims 1-12, wherein the kitchen appliance is one of a noodle maker, dough maker or bread maker. 14. A method (200) of controlling a flour product-based food preparation process, the method comprising: measuring (220) a dielectric property of the flour product during the food preparation process; determining (230) an initial moisture content of the flour product from the measured dielectric property; and generating a control signal (240) for adding water to the flour product as a function of said determined initial moisture content. 15. The method (200) of claim 14, further comprising: determining the amount of water bound to the flour product; and generating a further control signal for controlling an amount of power supplied to a heating unit (150) as a function of the determined amount of bound water.
